expectations.evidence: Replaces std::vector with 'static SpscRingBuffer<SensorSample, RING_BUF_CAPACITY> s_sample_ring' where RING_BUF_CAPACITY = 2048 (power of 2). The SpscRingBuffer uses 'std::array<T, N> buf_{}' for static storage. States: 'Zero std::vector.'

Replaces std::string with 'static char s_csv_buf[CSV_BUF_SIZE]' and snprintf: 'snprintf(s_csv_buf + buf_offset, CSV_BUF_SIZE - buf_offset, "%lld,%u,%u,%u\n", ...)'. States: 'No std::string. No std::stringstream.'

SpscRingBuffer has 'static_assert((N & (N - 1)) == 0, "Capacity must be a power of 2")' and 'static constexpr size_t MASK = N - 1'. Uses bitmask indexing: '(head + 1) & MASK' and '(tail + 1) & MASK'. RING_BUF_CAPACITY = 2048 (power of 2).

health_monitor_task uses ESP-IDF functions: 'heap_caps_get_free_size(MALLOC_CAP_8BIT)', 'heap_caps_get_largest_free_block(MALLOC_CAP_8BIT)', 'heap_caps_get_minimum_free_size(MALLOC_CAP_8BIT)'. Initial monitoring also uses 'heap_caps_get_free_size'.

ETL (Embedded Template Library) is not mentioned by name in the response. The response implements its own SpscRingBuffer instead of recommending etl::queue or etl::vector. No specific ETL container types are cited.

The response does not discuss ESP32-S3 memory regions (DRAM vs PSRAM). The 'Key Design Decisions' section compares the designs but does not mention PSRAM, DRAM, IRAM, or RTC memory regions. The compile flags section briefly mentions '-fno-exceptions' but no memory region discussion.

Provides complete implementation across sensor_types.h, spsc_ring_buffer.h, and data_logger.cpp including full app_main(), adc_reader_task(), logger_task(), health_monitor_task(), timer init, ADC init, and SD card writer with snprintf-based CSV formatting.
